sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: KVC Westerlo have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 1 win, 2 draws, and 2 losses, including a high-scoring 5-5 draw against Club Brugge. In contrast, FCV Dender are in poor form, winless in their last 5 games, with 1 draw and 4 losses, struggling to find the back of the net.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, KVC Westerlo and FCV Dender each secured a home win. Westerlo won 4-2 in May 2025, showcasing attacking prowess, while Dender edged out a 1-0 victory in March 2025, indicating their defensive resilience at home.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Westerlo, sitting 10th with 14 points, boast a strong attack (18 goals, 2nd in the league) but leaky defense (20 conceded, 15th). Dender, languishing at 16th with just 3 points, have struggled offensively (4 goals) and defensively (18 conceded), highlighting their overall poor campaign.

sections.subtitle: Key Insights
sections.text: Westerlo’s ability to create big chances (28, 5th in league) and their dominance in aerial duels (55.7% won, 2nd) could exploit Dender’s defensive frailties. Dender’s inability to convert big chances (evident in recent misses) and poor away form make them underdogs.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: With Sofascore predicting a 71% chance of a home win, Westerlo are clear favorites at Het Kuipje. Expect them to dominate possession and create scoring opportunities, while Dender will likely focus on counterattacks but may struggle to keep up.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between KVC Westerlo and FCV Dender ended with a 0-1 scoreline in favor of the away side. FCV Dender struck early with a goal from David Toševski in the 3rd minute, setting the tone for a defensive battle. Westerlo, despite trailing, showed more intent in possession and territorial dominance, while Dender relied on a compact setup to protect their lead. The home side created several opportunities but failed to convert, whereas Dender capitalized on their early chance to take the advantage into the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The attack momentum throughout the first half showed a clear shift between the two teams at various stages. Westerlo started brightly with some early pressure before Dender surged forward to claim their goal. Following the goal, there was a noticeable swing back to Westerlo as they pushed hard to equalize with sustained attacking spells, particularly in the middle of the half. Dender had brief moments of threat on the counter, but the majority of the momentum favored Westerlo as the half progressed, though they couldn’t find the finishing touch. The final minutes saw a slight resurgence from Dender, balancing out the attacking threat just before the whistle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Looking at the half-time statistics, KVC Westerlo held a slight edge in ball possession and dominated in terms of entries into the final third and penalty area, indicating their intent to attack. They created a big chance but missed it, alongside hitting the woodwork, showing they were close to leveling the score. On the other hand, FCV Dender were more clinical, converting their sole big chance into a goal. Dender also showed strength in aerial duels and overall duels won, which helped them maintain a solid defensive shape despite Westerlo’s pressure. The expected goals metric suggests Westerlo had the better chances overall, but their inability to finish kept them behind.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Player performances
halftimeSections.text: For KVC Westerlo, Doĝucan Haspolat stood out with his creative contribution, creating a big chance and delivering key passes in midfield. Isa Sakamoto and Nacho Ferri also showed promise in attack, with both registering shots on target and posing a threat. On the FCV Dender side, David Toševski was the standout performer, not only scoring the decisive goal but also maintaining a high rating for his overall impact. Junior Marsoni Sambu Mansoni contributed with an assist, playing a key role in the build-up to the goal. Dender’s goalkeeper Guillaume Dietsch also earned a strong rating for his efforts in keeping Westerlo at bay.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Heading into the second half, KVC Westerlo are likely to continue their attacking approach as they chase an equalizer. Given their dominance in possession and territorial stats, they will push for more chances, potentially exploiting set-pieces with their higher corner count. FCV Dender, on the other hand, will aim to defend their lead with a compact structure while looking for counter-attacking opportunities to extend their advantage. Westerlo’s urgency could leave gaps at the back, which Dender might target if they maintain their clinical edge. The second half could see a more open game if Westerlo commit numbers forward, but Dender’s defensive resilience will be tested.
